rationales.hook_strength: 'Stopped working for one reason' is specific and curiosity-driving, but 'one reason' is a mild oversell that edges toward clickbait.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is a single, actionable idea with a clear mechanical through-line from stimulus to record to recovery.
rationales.audience_fit: Busy 25-year-olds who lift casually will recognize the plateau frustration, though the logging angle assumes more gym commitment than some in this cohort have.
rationales.novelty: Progressive overload is well-trodden gym content; the 'soreness isn't the goal' reframe adds a minor fresh angle but won't surprise anyone who follows fitness accounts.
rationales.brand_fit: Voice is plain and direct, health claims are mechanistic not guaranteed, CTA ties all three tracked metrics together naturally, zero cringe phrasing.
rationales.predicted_performance: Strong saves from gym-goers already on this path, but the topic ceiling is moderate — progressive overload carousels are saturated and this won't break out of the fitness silo.
